title: Screening cloud reduces bare Coulomb repulsion
summary: The medium responds to a charged perturbation and softens the bare interaction, which is why W is milder than v in GW.
assumptions: Collective medium response is a useful intuition for why screened interactions differ from bare ones.

boundary_conditions: screening remains a meaningful language for the system
exclusions: regimes where a local atomic picture dominates instead

applies_to: explaining why W enters the GW self-energy instead of the bare interaction

promotion.rationale: Manual L2 seed for reusable GW/QSGW excitation memory.

payload.picture: Electronic polarization partially cancels the bare perturbation, so the effective interaction is dynamically reduced.
known_limits: does not by itself determine when the RPA-level description is sufficient
